text: A new, highly efficient and atom-economical route to benzoxepine derivatives of considerable importance, achieved by the formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition of o-vinylphenols and alkynes, is described. Employing a catalytic mixture of the rhodium compound [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2 in the reaction between the substrate and alkyne allows for selective activation of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl part of the substrate. Additionally, by using carbon monoxide instead of an alkyne in this reaction, formal (5 + 1) cycloaddition to the coumarin derivatives with various substituents is also achieved.
